scope_note: QA Madelung Anomaly Boundary Cert family [222]: extends [220] Madelung d-ordering to account for known anomalies. Structural claim: every known Madelung anomaly in a neutral-atom ground state (20 total: Cr, Cu, Nb, Mo, Ru, Rh, Pd, Ag, La, Ce, Gd, Pt, Au, Ac, Th, Pa, U, Np, Cm, Lr) satisfies |d(source) - d(destination)| <= 1 under (b, e) = (n, l). 10 anomalies are intra-d-class (|Δd|=0, f↔d in lanthanides/actinides, d↔p in Lr) and 10 are inter-adjacent-d-class (|Δd|=1, s↔d across boundaries). Null test (uniform random 2-subshell pairs from first 20 Madelung positions): boundary zone |Δd|<=1 contains 36.8% of pairs; observed 100% (20/20); enrichment 2.71x; binomial p = 2.1e-9. QA provides structural BOUNDARY (necessary for anomaly) but not sufficient condition (exchange/correlation physics determines WHICH zone members actually anomalise). Falsifiable: newly-discovered anomaly with |Δd|>=2 would break the claim. Ti, V, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni (d=4↔5) and Ta, W, Re, Os, Ir (d=6↔7) are in the permissive zone but follow Madelung. Does NOT explain half/full-shell stabilisation, two-electron Pd promotion, lanthanide clustering, relativistic Pt/Au — all SVP/permissibility semantic layer. Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-13. NIST atomic spectra database + Sato 2015 (Lr 7p1 measurement). Theory: docs/theory/QA_MADELUNG_ANOMALY_BOUNDARY.md. Checks MAB_1+ANOMALIES/MAPPING/ZONE/COVERAGE/NULL/COUNTEREX/SRC/WITNESS/F; 1 PASS + 1 FAIL; self-test ok
